prompt.instructions:
You are updating a person profile for: {{entity_id}}
Based on the existing profile (if any), new information from trigger files, and recent organizational context from digests, create an updated person profile.
The profile should be written from the organization's perspective - tracking who people are, their roles, and their involvement in organizational activities.
REQUIRED STRUCTURE:
1. Start with YAML frontmatter in standard format (enclosed in --- delimiters) containing: - name: Full name of the person - title: Their current title/role - department: Their department or team (if internal) - company: Their company (if external) - contact: Email or other contact info (if available) - projects: List of project IDs they're involved in - teams: List of team IDs they belong to or work with - last_seen: Most recent mention date
2. Then include these sections:
# [Person's Full Name]
## Current Role & Responsibilities A brief paragraph about their current position and key responsibilities. Include any recent role changes or new responsibilities.
## Project Involvement List ONLY projects this person is actually tied to — via a typed relationship (e.g. works_on, leads) or work they personally performed. Do not list a project merely because it was discussed in a meeting they attended. - Project Name: Their specific role or contribution
## Recent Activities Bullet points of activities THIS PERSON personally performed, decided, or owns. The <grounded_facts> block (their Recent Signals + typed relationships) is the AUTHORITATIVE source for attribution — derive activities from it, not from un-attributed transcript text. Meetings have multiple participants — do NOT attribute another participant's decisions, action items, or work to this person. If a decision reads "Jordan will…", it belongs to Jordan, not to others who merely attended. When unsure who did something, omit it.
## Key Relationships Note working relationships ONLY when grounded in a typed relationship or an explicit statement (never inferred from co-attendance alone): - Works closely with: [person names] - Reports to: [if known] - Direct reports: [if applicable]
## Expertise & Skills Areas where they've demonstrated expertise or been recognized for their knowledge.
## Notes & Context Any other relevant information about this person that helps understand their role in the organization or their working style.
IMPORTANT GUIDELINES: - CORRECTIONS OVERRIDE EVERYTHING: if the <grounded_facts> block contains a "Corrections (authoritative …)" section, treat each correction as the final truth. It overrides the existing profile, the trigger files, and any derived fact. Never re-introduce a fact a correction contradicts. - Use the exact entity_id provided (e.g., "person-sarah-chen") - Focus on professional/organizational context - Don't make up information not found in the source documents - ATTRIBUTION IS CRITICAL: this profile is for {{entity_id}} ONLY. Trigger files are shared meetings involving many people. Attribute an activity, decision, action item, or responsibility to this person ONLY when they are the named actor/owner (per the "Recent Signals"/grounded facts) or it is explicitly stated about them. Never carry a co-participant's work onto this profile. Prefer omission over a guess. - A fact stated about this person by someone else (e.g. "X leads Project Y") may be included ONLY when backed by a typed relationship or an explicit statement — do not infer leadership/ownership from mere co-attendance. - If updating an existing profile, preserve important historical information - Track actual activities and contributions, not speculation - Use person IDs consistently (e.g., "person-firstname-lastname") - For external people, note their company/organization clearly - Avoid generic example content - use actual information from the files
